name: Tourte Milanese
recipeInstructions:
Note: Spinach should be blanched first and then well drained. Red peppers should be cut into 1-inch pieces and blanched.
Lightly grease an 8-inch springform pan. Roll out three-fourths of the pastry to a thickness of one-quarter inch and line the bottom and sides of the pan. Keep the remaining pastry refrigerated. Heat oil and butter in a large skillet over medium heat until hot.
Add spinach and garlic, sauté for 2 to 3 minutes, season with nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Remove from the skillet. Add red peppers and sauté briefly before removing them from heat and setting aside.
For omelets: Lightly beat eggs, herbs, and salt in a bowl. In an 8-inch skillet over medium heat, add one tablespoon butter and distribute it evenly on the bottom of the pan. Pour in half the egg mixture and gently stir. As the eggs start to set, lift the edges to allow the uncooked liquid underneath to flow.
When the eggs are completely set and the top is no longer moist, shake the pan and carefully slide the omelet onto a warm plate. Repeat with the remaining butter and egg mixture.
To assemble: Position the rack in the lower one-third of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Layer ingredients in the prepared pan in the following order: one omelet, half spinach, half cheese, half ham, and all red peppers. Repeat layering in reverse using the remaining ingredients. Roll the remaining pastry to a thickness of one-quarter inch, cut out an 8-inch circle, place it over the omelet, and seal well to the pastry lining by pinching with fingers.
With the tip of a knife, draw the desired number of slices directly on the pastry or decorate with scraps of pastry. Brush lightly with beaten egg. Place the pan on the baking sheet and bake until golden brown, about 70 to 75 minutes. Cool slightly before serving the tourte warm, slicing with a sharp, thin knife.
